sources.name: Kenshiki defense registry product surface (AI-generated copy, routing, guardrails)
sources.license: Kenshiki Labs first-party work; repository MIT license.
sources.transform_notes: Verbatim projection of the AI-generated product surface: top-level ui/modes/categories/renderer/fields/statuses copy blocks plus the complete destination records (nextSteps, whyMatters, harmProfile rationale, aiGuidance guardrails, renderer and prestage config) and routing structures (situations, topRecommendationIds, destinationOrder, badgeSchema, harmProfileMeta, primitiveDecisionPolicy). Destinations re-keyed in sorted order for determinism; no content changes. See scripts/normalize-intelligence.mjs.

review.notes: AI-generated product surface under SSOT governance. Models reformat these structured facts; they must not invent destinations, harm ratings, or legal claims (defense_kenshiki_spec.md).

ui.title: Defense
ui.instruction: Real steps you can take, in order — mark each one done as you go.
ui.openHint: Shows why this defense action matters and how to complete it.
ui.copyHint: Copies this field placeholder to the clipboard for one minute.
ui.sealBody: Paste a confirmation number if you have one, or mark it done without evidence. Either way, Pulse records the state locally.
ui.dashboardBody: Every destination is information + handoff today. The state model is ready for direct API renderers later.
ui.destinationReadBody: Open the destination, follow the guide, then record what happened.
ui.situationReadBody: Each destination is renderer-agnostic: website today, direct API later.
ui.defaultReadBody: Pulse keeps proof local. Defense turns continuity into outside actions you can complete and seal.

reduceExposure.title: Reduce exposure
reduceExposure.subtitle: Lower your attack surface before something happens.

respondToThreat.title: Respond to a threat
respondToThreat.subtitle: Freeze, report, and seal what you did.

situations.title: Get my info off people-search sites
situations.subtitle: Start with the brokers most likely to expose address and profile data.

situations.title: Deep clean 50+ data brokers
situations.subtitle: Work through the broader broker catalog when you need a deeper clean.

situations.title: Hide my address from public records
situations.subtitle: Reduce exposure from property, voter, mail, and DMV records.

situations.title: Lock down my social profiles
situations.subtitle: Tighten public profile and discovery settings.

situations.title: See what is already exposed
situations.subtitle: Check breach exposure before rotating credentials.

situations.title: Audit my app permissions
situations.subtitle: Review account and device permissions that can leak recovery signals.

situations.title: Lock down my credit
situations.subtitle: Freeze bureaus and adjacent account-opening systems.

situations.title: Someone opened an account I didn't authorize
situations.subtitle: Create a recovery record, then dispute the account with the right system.

situations.title: My phone number got hijacked
situations.subtitle: Carrier fraud line, port-out PIN, NCTUE, and bureau freezes.

situations.title: My tax refund was stolen or rejected
situations.subtitle: IRS IP PIN, FTC report, and freeze follow-through.

situations.title: I see something wrong on my credit report
situations.subtitle: Dispute incorrect bureau records and seal the case numbers.

situations.title: I don't know what happened
situations.subtitle: Start with the FTC recovery plan, then branch into the right actions.

situations.title: Stop browser tracking at the source
situations.subtitle: Block trackers, cross-site profiling, and ad-tech collection before data leaves your browser.

situations.title: Lock down phone privacy settings
situations.subtitle: Tighten iOS and Android settings that limit ad, location, app, and permission data collection.

situations.title: Block tracking at the network layer
situations.subtitle: Use DNS or local filtering to reduce tracker calls across apps and devices.

situations.title: Reduce account-linking and credential reuse
situations.subtitle: Use password managers and aliases to prevent one leak from linking every account.

situations.title: Use a removal service if DIY is too much
situations.subtitle: Compare paid and freemium services that keep re-running broker removals over time.

sourceBadges.badbool_high_priority: BADBOOL marks this as high priority.
sourceBadges.badbool_requires_drivers_license: BADBOOL indicates the flow may require a driver license or ID document.
sourceBadges.badbool_requires_phone_call: BADBOOL indicates a phone call may be required.
sourceBadges.badbool_fee_or_paid_access: BADBOOL indicates paid access or a fee may be involved.

harmProfile.description: Evidence-based assessment of a destination's role in the consumer data ecosystem. Distinguishes defenders (bureaus in freeze role, government agencies, privacy tools) from data collectors and from sanctioned bad actors. All harm claims must cite a public regulatory action, court filing, or major journalism source.

actorClass.description: Functional role of the entity in the data ecosystem.

harmSeverity.description: none: defender or privacy tool. low: routine commercial use with consumer controls. moderate: commercial broker; data sold but no major enforcement. high: major breach, settlement, or repeated state AG action. severe: federal enforcement (FTC/FCC/CFPB) for unfair/deceptive practices.

harmRationale.description: One-sentence factual basis for the severity rating.

consumerBenefit.description: How this destination helps the consumer (null for non-defender destinations).

harmProfileMeta.populationStrategy: All 105 destinations populated with evidence-based harmProfile. Defender flows (bureau freezes/disputes, government agencies, carrier fraud lines, privacy tools) are rated severity:none/low with explicit consumerBenefit. Commercial brokers and platforms rated moderate/high/severe with documentedHarms citing FTC, FCC, CFPB, state AG actions, or major journalism.

userFacingPrimitiveCriteria: user can take a direct action today
action reduces exposure, prevents collection, detects breach exposure, or creates recovery proof
action can be sealed locally with userMarkDone, userPaste, userPhoto, or userPasteOrPhoto
source URL or phone handoff can be verified or refreshed

collapseRules.example: PeopleConnect brands collapse into one suppression action when the parent portal covers all brands.

collapseRules.pattern: upstream wholesale data source with no consumer self-service action
collapseRules.decision: reference intelligence unless a freeze, dispute, restriction, or opt-out exists
jurisdiction-aware primitive requiring current official sources
prevention primitive for browser, mobile OS, DNS, credential, alias, or permission controls
candidate intelligence only until actionability, parent-network ownership, opt-out target, and source currency are verified
collapseRules.example: A broker with multiple state rows should collapse to one company/action candidate keyed by GroupUUID_Combined.

referenceIntelligence.purpose: Reference intelligence for deciding which supply-chain actors become user-facing Defense primitives.
referenceIntelligence.productionRule: Do not expose every raw company as a primitive; collapse parent networks, prioritize actionable root-cause defenses, and mark purely informational actors as reference-only.
referenceIntelligence.dataBrokerRegistryPurpose: Raw state data-broker registry source for candidate discovery, opt-out URL enrichment, and jurisdiction/source lookup. Not every row becomes a user-facing primitive.
referenceIntelligence.badboolPurpose: Reference only for priority coverage and manual verification; do not copy prose into shipped app content without licensing review.
referenceIntelligence.badboolBadgePolicy: BADBOOL symbols are used only as normalized metadata hints; shipped instructions remain original Kenshiki copy.

adguard_dns_setup.whyMatters: AdGuard DNS can block ads and trackers at the DNS layer across browsers and apps.
nextSteps: Open AdGuard DNS.
Choose free or personal DNS setup.
Configure device or router DNS and mark done.
adguard_dns_setup.trustTradeoff: moves DNS query visibility to the DNS provider you choose

harmProfile.harmRationale: AdGuard's free public DNS resolver with tracker and ad filtering.
harmProfile.consumerBenefit: Network-level ad and tracker blocking; free public DNS.

advanced_background_checks_optout.whyMatters: Advanced Background Checks is prioritized by public opt-out references as a people-search or broker exposure to check and remove when present.
nextSteps: Open the Advanced Background Checks opt-out or privacy page.
Search only for records that appear to match you.
Submit the removal request, confirm email if required, and mark done after the request is submitted.
advanced_background_checks_optout.sourceAttribution: BADBOOL priority reference; verify against destination site
advanced_background_checks_optout.trustTradeoff: may require giving identity details or email address to opt out of broker records

harmProfile.harmRationale: Free background-check site; shares lineage with SmartBackgroundChecks.

ancestry_content_removal.whyMatters: Ancestry.com is prioritized by public opt-out references as a people-search or broker exposure to check and remove when present.
nextSteps: Open the Ancestry.com opt-out or privacy page.
Search only for records that appear to match you.
Submit the removal request, confirm email if required, and mark done after the request is submitted.
ancestry_content_removal.sourceAttribution: BADBOOL priority reference; verify against destination site
ancestry_content_removal.trustTradeoff: may require giving identity details or email address to opt out of broker records

harmProfile.harmRationale: Ancestry holds 22M+ DNA samples and extensive genealogy records; data is monetized through research partnerships under opt-in consent.

documentedHarms.summary: Ancestry shares de-identified genetic data with research partners under opt-in; subject to scrutiny over consent durability after Blackstone acquisition in 2020.

android_delete_ad_id.displayName: Delete or reset Android advertising ID
android_delete_ad_id.whyMatters: Deleting or resetting the Android advertising ID reduces app-level advertising linkage.
nextSteps: Open Settings → Privacy → Ads, or Google settings depending on device.
Delete or reset the advertising ID if available.
Turn off ad personalization where available.
android_delete_ad_id.trustTradeoff: uses Android and Google privacy controls

harmProfile.harmRationale: Resets/deletes Android Advertising ID; defender flow within Google's broader ad ecosystem.

documentedHarms.summary: ~80% of Alphabet revenue is advertising built on cross-product behavioral data; multiple antitrust suits in progress.

harmProfile.consumerBenefit: Breaks the persistent advertising identifier used by ad-tech across apps.

android_privacy_dashboard_audit.whyMatters: Android Privacy Dashboard shows recent app access to camera, microphone, location, and other sensitive permissions.
nextSteps: Open Settings → Privacy → Privacy Dashboard.
Review recent sensitive permission access.
Revoke permissions that are not needed.
android_privacy_dashboard_audit.trustTradeoff: requires reviewing local Android app permissions

harmProfile.harmRationale: Android's parent (Google) has a high-harm advertising business; Privacy Dashboard is nonetheless a useful defender flow.

documentedHarms.summary: ~80% of Alphabet revenue is advertising built on cross-product behavioral data; multiple antitrust suits in progress.

harmProfile.consumerBenefit: Shows which apps accessed location, camera, microphone, and contacts in the last 24 hours.

apple_id_security.whyMatters: Apple ID security controls protect device recovery, passkeys, and account access.
nextSteps: Open Apple ID account management.
Review trusted devices, phone numbers, and recovery settings.
Mark done after the account is secured.
apple_id_security.trustTradeoff: requires reviewing account or device permissions

harmProfile.harmRationale: Apple has positioned privacy as a competitive differentiator; ATT framework limits third-party tracking; Apple does not sell user data to advertisers.

documentedHarms.summary: Apple's App Tracking Transparency (iOS 14.5) requires explicit opt-in for cross-app tracking; positioned privacy as a differentiator. Apple does not sell user data to third-party advertisers.

harmProfile.consumerBenefit: Audit account security, sign-in devices, and connected apps; defender flow.

att_port_out_pin.whyMatters: An AT&T transfer PIN helps block unauthorized number transfers.
nextSteps: Open AT&T transfer PIN help.
Generate or review the transfer PIN.
Mark done after the PIN is secured.
att_port_out_pin.trustTradeoff: shares account and phone details with mobile carrier support

harmProfile.harmRationale: Carrier defensive flow (fraud line / port-out PIN) protects the user; note the carrier separately faced FCC fines in 2024 for unrelated location-data sharing.

documentedHarms.summary: FCC fined AT&T $57M for illegally sharing customer location data.

harmProfile.consumerBenefit: Required PIN/passcode prevents unauthorized port-outs; #1 defense against SIM swap.

bitwarden_password_manager.whyMatters: A password manager reduces credential reuse, which makes breached data less useful for account takeover.
nextSteps: Open Bitwarden.
Create or sign in to your vault.
Move high-risk accounts to unique passwords and mark done.
bitwarden_password_manager.trustTradeoff: stores encrypted password vault with password manager provider or self-hosted option

harmProfile.harmRationale: Open-source password manager with audited zero-knowledge architecture; non-profit-friendly pricing.
harmProfile.consumerBenefit: End-to-end-encrypted password storage; defeats credential stuffing and reuse attacks.

brave_browser.whyMatters: Brave ships with built-in tracker, ad, fingerprinting, and cookie protections.
nextSteps: Install Brave on the device you browse from.
Set Shields to standard or aggressive based on site compatibility.
Make it your default browser if you want ongoing protection.
brave_browser.trustTradeoff: requires moving browsing activity into a privacy-focused third-party browser

harmProfile.harmRationale: Privacy-focused browser; one 2020 incident where Brave auto-inserted crypto affiliate codes (later changed).

documentedHarms.summary: Brave was caught auto-inserting affiliate referral codes on cryptocurrency URLs; the company apologized and changed default behavior.

harmProfile.consumerBenefit: Built-in tracker and ad blocking; Tor windows; aggressive default fingerprinting protection.

carrier_fraud_call_att.whyMatters: AT&T fraud support can review unauthorized SIM or number-transfer activity.
nextSteps: Call the fraud line.
Ask for account takeover review.
Request wireless account protection.
carrier_fraud_call_att.trustTradeoff: shares account and phone details with mobile carrier support

harmProfile.harmRationale: Carrier defensive flow (fraud line / port-out PIN) protects the user; note the carrier separately faced FCC fines in 2024 for unrelated location-data sharing.

documentedHarms.summary: FCC fined AT&T $57M for illegally sharing customer location data.

harmProfile.consumerBenefit: Direct line to AT&T fraud team; can lock SIM swap and port-out within minutes.

carrier_fraud_call_tmobile.whyMatters: Carrier fraud teams can lock down port-out risk after a SIM hijack.
nextSteps: Call the fraud line.
Ask for account takeover review.
Request a port-out PIN or account lock.
carrier_fraud_call_tmobile.trustTradeoff: shares account and phone details with mobile carrier support

harmProfile.harmRationale: Carrier defensive flow (fraud line / port-out PIN) protects the user; note the carrier separately faced FCC fines in 2024 for unrelated location-data sharing.

documentedHarms.summary: FCC fined T-Mobile $80M (largest of the four) for illegally sharing customer location data, including Sprint pre-merger conduct.

harmProfile.consumerBenefit: Direct line to T-Mobile fraud team; can lock SIM swap and port-out within minutes.

carrier_fraud_call_verizon.whyMatters: Verizon fraud support can review unauthorized SIM or number-transfer activity.
nextSteps: Call the fraud line.
Ask for account takeover review.
Request port protection or account lock.
carrier_fraud_call_verizon.trustTradeoff: shares account and phone details with mobile carrier support

harmProfile.harmRationale: Carrier defensive flow (fraud line / port-out PIN) protects the user; note the carrier separately faced FCC fines in 2024 for unrelated location-data sharing.

documentedHarms.summary: FCC fined Verizon $46.9M for illegally sharing customer location data.

harmProfile.consumerBenefit: Direct line to Verizon fraud team; can lock SIM swap and port-out within minutes.

chexsystems_freeze.whyMatters: ChexSystems freezes can reduce fraudulent bank-account opening in your name.
nextSteps: Open the ChexSystems freeze page.
Verify your identity.
Place the freeze and keep the PIN or confirmation.
chexsystems_freeze.trustTradeoff: shares sensitive identity data with regulated consumer reporting agency

harmProfile.harmRationale: Specialty banking CRA; freezing prevents fraudulent deposit account openings.
harmProfile.consumerBenefit: Blocks fraudulent checking/savings account openings; free.

clustal_optout.whyMatters: Clustal is prioritized by public opt-out references as a people-search or broker exposure to check and remove when present.
nextSteps: Open the Clustal opt-out or privacy page.
Search only for records that appear to match you.
Submit the removal request, confirm email if required, and mark done after the request is submitted.
clustal_optout.sourceAttribution: BADBOOL priority reference; verify against destination site
clustal_optout.trustTradeoff: may require giving identity details or email address to opt out of broker records

harmProfile.harmRationale: Smaller people-search aggregator.

clustrmaps_optout.whyMatters: ClustrMaps is prioritized by public opt-out references as a people-search or broker exposure to check and remove when present.
nextSteps: Open the ClustrMaps opt-out or privacy page.
Search only for records that appear to match you.
Submit the removal request, confirm email if required, and mark done after the request is submitted.
clustrmaps_optout.sourceAttribution: BADBOOL priority reference; verify against destination site
clustrmaps_optout.trustTradeoff: may require giving identity details or email address to opt out of broker records

harmProfile.harmRationale: Address and relatives aggregator; widely indexed by search engines.

cms_medicare_account.whyMatters: A claimed Medicare account helps prevent medical identity misuse through an unclaimed portal.
nextSteps: Open Medicare account login.
Sign in or create your account.
Review profile security and mark done.
cms_medicare_account.trustTradeoff: shares identity or incident facts with official government service

harmProfile.harmRationale: Federal/state agency acting in a defensive or protective role for the consumer.
harmProfile.consumerBenefit: Locks your Medicare account against fraudulent providers billing in your name.

deleteme_service.whyMatters: DeleteMe performs ongoing removals across many broker and people-search sites.
nextSteps: Open the service site.
Review what identity data it requires and the trust tradeoff.
Start the scan or removal workflow only if you are comfortable sharing the required details.
deleteme_service.trustTradeoff: requires sharing identity details with a data removal service

harmProfile.harmRationale: One of the original commercial removal services; ~$129/year; partial coverage per independent testing.

documentedHarms.summary: PCMag testing found even the best removal services achieved only partial coverage; results vary by service.

harmProfile.consumerBenefit: Quarterly removal reports across ~30+ major brokers; subscription required.

dppa_dmv_optout_info.whyMatters: DPPA-related DMV privacy choices can reduce exposure of motor-vehicle record data.
nextSteps: Open your state motor vehicle services directory.
Find privacy, records, or opt-out instructions.
Mark done after you record the state-specific next step.
dppa_dmv_optout_info.trustTradeoff: may require jurisdiction-specific forms or eligibility details

harmProfile.harmRationale: Federal DPPA gives consumers the right to opt out of DMV record disclosure for marketing.
harmProfile.consumerBenefit: Blocks DMV resale of your driver record for marketing purposes (DPPA 18 USC § 2721).

duckduckgo_app_tracking_protection.whyMatters: DuckDuckGo App Tracking Protection can block many third-party tracker calls from Android apps.
nextSteps: Open DuckDuckGo App Tracking Protection information.
Install DuckDuckGo on Android.
Enable App Tracking Protection and mark done.
duckduckgo_app_tracking_protection.trustTradeoff: uses a local VPN-style blocker, so DuckDuckGo handles tracker blocking on device

harmProfile.harmRationale: Third-party VPN-style tracker blocking; same MS-tracker concern noted in browser entry, though scope is different.

documentedHarms.summary: DuckDuckGo browser was found to allow Microsoft trackers under a search syndication contract; DDG later removed the exception.

harmProfile.consumerBenefit: Blocks third-party trackers across all apps on Android (uses local VPN; no data sent to DDG).

duckduckgo_privacy_essentials.whyMatters: DuckDuckGo browser and extensions block trackers and can reduce app or web tracking depending on platform.
nextSteps: Open DuckDuckGo app or extension page.
Install it on your primary browser or phone.
Enable tracker protections and mark done.
duckduckgo_privacy_essentials.trustTradeoff: requires trusting DuckDuckGo browser or extension with browsing protection

harmProfile.harmRationale: Privacy-focused browser/extension; in 2022 was found to allow Microsoft trackers under a search syndication contract (later removed).

documentedHarms.summary: DuckDuckGo browser was found to allow Microsoft trackers under a search syndication contract; DDG later removed the exception.

harmProfile.consumerBenefit: Tracker blocking, smarter encryption, and privacy grade ratings.

early_warning_freeze.whyMatters: Early Warning Services supports bank-account risk checks, so it matters for unauthorized deposit accounts.
nextSteps: Open Early Warning consumer information.
Request the appropriate consumer file or restriction.
Save the case or confirmation number.
early_warning_freeze.trustTradeoff: shares sensitive identity data with regulated consumer reporting agency

harmProfile.harmRationale: Bank-consortium CRA for deposit accounts; freeze prevents fraudulent account openings.
harmProfile.consumerBenefit: Blocks deposit-account fraud; free.

easyoptouts_service.whyMatters: EasyOptOuts offers lower-cost automated removals across many data broker sites.
nextSteps: Open the service site.
Review what identity data it requires and the trust tradeoff.
Start the scan or removal workflow only if you are comfortable sharing the required details.
easyoptouts_service.trustTradeoff: requires sharing identity details with a data removal service

harmProfile.harmRationale: Lower-cost commercial removal service (~$20/year); recommended by Yael Grauer's Big Ass Data Broker Opt-Out List.

documentedHarms.summary: PCMag testing found even the best removal services achieved only partial coverage; results vary by service.

harmProfile.consumerBenefit: Lower-cost subscription alternative; recommended by independent privacy journalists.

equifax_dispute.whyMatters: Use Equifax disputes to challenge inaccurate Equifax report entries.
nextSteps: Open the Equifax dispute portal.
Select the incorrect item.
Save the dispute confirmation number.
equifax_dispute.trustTradeoff: shares sensitive identity data with regulated consumer reporting agency

harmProfile.harmRationale: Acting in defensive capacity for the consumer; freeze/dispute rights are federally mandated under FCRA and are free. The parent company's broader data-broker activities are not part of this defensive flow.

documentedHarms.summary: Breach exposed sensitive data of 147M consumers; $575M FTC/CFPB/states settlement (2019).

harmProfile.consumerBenefit: Correct inaccurate or fraudulent items on your Equifax credit file; free FCRA right.

equifax_freeze.whyMatters: Freezing Equifax helps prevent new credit from being opened in your name.
nextSteps: Open the Equifax freeze page.
Create or sign in to your account.
Place a security freeze and save the confirmation.
equifax_freeze.trustTradeoff: shares sensitive identity data with regulated consumer reporting agency

harmProfile.harmRationale: Acting in defensive capacity for the consumer; freeze/dispute rights are federally mandated under FCRA and are free. The parent company's broader data-broker activities are not part of this defensive flow.

documentedHarms.summary: Breach exposed sensitive data of 147M consumers; $575M FTC/CFPB/states settlement (2019).

harmProfile.consumerBenefit: Blocks new-account fraud at one of the three primary credit bureaus; free under FCRA.

experian_dispute.whyMatters: Use Experian disputes to challenge inaccurate Experian report entries.
nextSteps: Open the Experian dispute portal.
Select the incorrect item.
Save the dispute confirmation number.
experian_dispute.trustTradeoff: shares sensitive identity data with regulated consumer reporting agency

harmProfile.harmRationale: Acting in defensive capacity for the consumer; freeze/dispute rights are federally mandated under FCRA and are free. The parent company's broader data-broker activities are not part of this defensive flow.
harmProfile.consumerBenefit: Correct inaccurate or fraudulent items on your Experian credit file; free FCRA right.

experian_freeze.whyMatters: Experian is one of the core credit files lenders check before issuing credit.
nextSteps: Open Experian Freeze Center.
Verify your identity.
Freeze the file and save the confirmation.
experian_freeze.trustTradeoff: shares sensitive identity data with regulated consumer reporting agency

harmProfile.harmRationale: Acting in defensive capacity for the consumer; freeze/dispute rights are federally mandated under FCRA and are free. The parent company's broader data-broker activities are not part of this defensive flow.
harmProfile.consumerBenefit: Blocks new-account fraud at one of the three primary credit bureaus; free under FCRA.

facebook_lockdown.whyMatters: Facebook profile data can expose recovery clues, social graph, or impersonation material.
nextSteps: Open Facebook privacy settings.
Limit public profile discovery.
Review contact, tag, and visibility settings.
facebook_lockdown.trustTradeoff: requires changing account settings on third-party platform

harmProfile.harmRationale: Meta's ad model depends on cross-site Pixel/Conversions API tracking. $5B FTC settlement in 2019 over Cambridge Analytica; ongoing EU GDPR enforcement.

documentedHarms.summary: Facebook paid $5B FTC penalty over Cambridge Analytica privacy violations.
~97% of Meta revenue is advertising built on cross-site Pixel and Conversions API tracking; multiple GDPR fines in EU.

harmProfile.consumerBenefit: Privacy lockdown reduces (but does not eliminate) Meta's behavioral profile of you.

firefox_enhanced_tracking_protection.whyMatters: Firefox Enhanced Tracking Protection blocks many social, cross-site, fingerprinting, and crypto-mining trackers.
nextSteps: Open Firefox privacy protections guidance.
Set Enhanced Tracking Protection to Standard or Strict.
Mark done after the setting is active.
firefox_enhanced_tracking_protection.trustTradeoff: requires using Firefox or configuring Firefox privacy settings

harmProfile.harmRationale: Mozilla Firefox's built-in tracker protection; maintained by a non-profit foundation.
harmProfile.consumerBenefit: Blocks third-party tracking cookies, fingerprinters, cryptominers, and known trackers.

ftc_identity_theft_report.whyMatters: The FTC report creates a recovery record you can use with bureaus and institutions.
nextSteps: Open IdentityTheft.gov.
Choose the closest situation.
Save the report or recovery plan number.
ftc_identity_theft_report.trustTradeoff: shares identity or incident facts with official government service

harmProfile.harmRationale: Federal/state agency acting in a defensive or protective role for the consumer.
harmProfile.consumerBenefit: Federal identity-theft report; required documentation for credit bureaus, banks, and law enforcement.

ftc_recovery_plan.whyMatters: The FTC recovery plan gives you a structured next-action list when the incident is unclear.
nextSteps: Open the FTC recovery plan assistant.
Answer the incident questions.
Use the plan to choose the next Defense actions.
ftc_recovery_plan.trustTradeoff: shares identity or incident facts with official government service

harmProfile.harmRationale: Federal/state agency acting in a defensive or protective role for the consumer.
harmProfile.consumerBenefit: FTC-issued step-by-step recovery checklist personalized to the user's reported incident.

ghostery_extension.whyMatters: Ghostery can block advertising and analytics trackers across supported browsers and mobile browsing surfaces.
nextSteps: Open Ghostery.
Install the browser extension or mobile browser option.
Enable tracker blocking and mark done.
ghostery_extension.trustTradeoff: third-party extension or browser can observe browsing activity to block trackers

harmProfile.harmRationale: Now owned by Cliqz/Burda; the historical 'GhostRank' aggregated tracker-data sales program was discontinued.

documentedHarms.summary: Historical concern: Ghostery's prior owner sold aggregated tracker data via 'GhostRank'; product was sold to Cliqz/Burda and the data-sale model was discontinued.

harmProfile.consumerBenefit: Blocks trackers; review settings to confirm no anonymized data sharing is enabled.

google_account_security.whyMatters: Google account security controls protect email, password recovery, and OAuth app access.
nextSteps: Open Google Security Checkup.
Review devices, recovery, and connected apps.
Mark done after risky access is removed.
google_account_security.trustTradeoff: requires reviewing account or device permissions

harmProfile.harmRationale: Google's ad model depends on cross-product behavioral data; YouTube paid $170M COPPA penalty; multiple antitrust suits in progress.

documentedHarms.summary: Google and YouTube paid $170M to FTC/NY AG to settle allegations they illegally collected children's data in violation of COPPA.
~80% of Alphabet revenue is advertising built on cross-product behavioral data; multiple antitrust suits in progress.

harmProfile.consumerBenefit: Audit ad personalization, location history, web activity, and connected third-party apps.

haveibeenpwned_check.whyMatters: Known breach exposure tells you which credentials deserve immediate rotation.
nextSteps: Open HaveIBeenPwned.
Check your primary email.
Rotate passwords for exposed services.
haveibeenpwned_check.trustTradeoff: requires checking email or identifier against breach service

harmProfile.harmRationale: Independent breach notification service operated by Troy Hunt; free, widely trusted by security community.
harmProfile.consumerBenefit: Tells you which of your accounts have appeared in known breaches; free.

icloud_hide_my_email.whyMatters: Email aliases reduce cross-site account linking and make broker enrichment harder.
nextSteps: Open Apple Hide My Email guidance.
Create aliases for high-risk signups where available.
Use unique aliases going forward and mark done.
icloud_hide_my_email.trustTradeoff: requires iCloud+ and routes alias email through Apple

harmProfile.harmRationale: Apple iCloud+ feature creating unique forwarding addresses; aligned with Apple's privacy posture.

documentedHarms.summary: Apple's App Tracking Transparency (iOS 14.5) requires explicit opt-in for cross-app tracking; positioned privacy as a differentiator. Apple does not sell user data to third-party advertisers.

harmProfile.consumerBenefit: Unique per-site email addresses prevent cross-site email-keyed identity linkage.

incogni_service.whyMatters: Incogni sends recurring removal requests to supported data brokers.
nextSteps: Open the service site.
Review what identity data it requires and the trust tradeoff.
Start the scan or removal workflow only if you are comfortable sharing the required details.
incogni_service.trustTradeoff: requires sharing identity details with a data removal service

harmProfile.harmRationale: Surfshark-affiliated commercial removal service (~$96/year); partial coverage per independent testing.

documentedHarms.summary: PCMag testing found even the best removal services achieved only partial coverage; results vary by service.

harmProfile.consumerBenefit: Automated removal across 100+ brokers; subscription required.

innovis_dispute.whyMatters: Use Innovis disputes to challenge inaccurate Innovis consumer report entries.
nextSteps: Open the Innovis dispute page.
Submit the incorrect item details.
Save the dispute confirmation number.
innovis_dispute.trustTradeoff: shares sensitive identity data with regulated consumer reporting agency

harmProfile.harmRationale: Acting in defensive capacity for the consumer; freeze/dispute rights are federally mandated under FCRA and are free. The parent company's broader data-broker activities are not part of this defensive flow.
harmProfile.consumerBenefit: Correct inaccurate or fraudulent items on your Innovis credit file; free FCRA right.

innovis_freeze.whyMatters: Innovis is a fourth national consumer reporting file that some creditors check.

harmProfile.harmRationale: iOS App Tracking Transparency is a defender flow; Apple does not sell user data.

harmProfile.consumerBenefit: Forces all apps to ask before tracking across other apps and websites.

harmProfile.harmRationale: Defender flow; the operating system gives the user direct control.

harmProfile.harmRationale: TikTok faced DOJ/FTC suit in 2024 for COPPA violations alleging illegal collection of children's data; also subject to federal divestiture legislation.

documentedHarms.summary: DOJ/FTC sued TikTok for COPPA violations alleging illegal collection of data on children under 13.

harmProfile.consumerBenefit: Privacy lockdown limits behavioral profiling; consider whether to keep the app installed at all.

tmobile_port_out_pin.whyMatters: A port-out PIN helps stop attackers from moving your number to another carrier.
nextSteps: Open T-Mobile transfer guidance.
Set or rotate the port-out PIN.
Mark done after the PIN is secured.
tmobile_port_out_pin.trustTradeoff: shares account and phone details with mobile carrier support

harmProfile.harmRationale: Carrier defensive flow (fraud line / port-out PIN) protects the user; note the carrier separately faced FCC fines in 2024 for unrelated location-data sharing.

documentedHarms.summary: FCC fined T-Mobile $80M (largest of the four) for illegally sharing customer location data, including Sprint pre-merger conduct.

harmProfile.consumerBenefit: Required PIN/passcode prevents unauthorized port-outs; #1 defense against SIM swap.

transunion_dispute.whyMatters: Use TransUnion disputes to challenge inaccurate TransUnion report entries.
nextSteps: Open the TransUnion dispute portal.
Select the incorrect item.
Save the dispute confirmation number.
transunion_dispute.trustTradeoff: shares sensitive identity data with regulated consumer reporting agency

harmProfile.harmRationale: Acting in defensive capacity for the consumer; freeze/dispute rights are federally mandated under FCRA and are free. The parent company's broader data-broker activities are not part of this defensive flow.
harmProfile.consumerBenefit: Correct inaccurate or fraudulent items on your TransUnion credit file; free FCRA right.

transunion_freeze.whyMatters: TransUnion freezes reduce the chance of new-account fraud.
nextSteps: Open TransUnion freeze.
Sign in or create an account.
Place the freeze and save the confirmation.
transunion_freeze.trustTradeoff: shares sensitive identity data with regulated consumer reporting agency

harmProfile.harmRationale: Acting in defensive capacity for the consumer; freeze/dispute rights are federally mandated under FCRA and are free. The parent company's broader data-broker activities are not part of this defensive flow.
harmProfile.consumerBenefit: Blocks new-account fraud at one of the three primary credit bureaus; free under FCRA.

ublock_origin_extension.whyMatters: uBlock Origin blocks many ads, trackers, and malicious domains before they load in the browser.
nextSteps: Open the uBlock Origin site or browser extension store.
Install it in the browser you actually use.
Leave the default filter lists on, then mark this done.
ublock_origin_extension.trustTradeoff: browser extension can inspect web requests and page context in supported browsers

harmProfile.harmRationale: Open-source, single-developer-maintained content blocker; widely considered the gold standard.
harmProfile.consumerBenefit: Blocks trackers, ads, and many fingerprinting techniques; free and open source.

usphonebook_optout.whyMatters: USPhoneBook is prioritized by public opt-out references as a people-search or broker exposure to check and remove when present.
nextSteps: Open the USPhoneBook opt-out or privacy page.
Search only for records that appear to match you.
Submit the removal request, confirm email if required, and mark done after the request is submitted.
usphonebook_optout.sourceAttribution: BADBOOL priority reference; verify against destination site
usphonebook_optout.trustTradeoff: may require giving identity details or email address to opt out of broker records

harmProfile.harmRationale: Phone-number-keyed people search aggregating address and contact data.

harmProfile.harmRationale: USPS service is a defender flow, but the program has been abused by thieves enrolling under stolen identities; claiming first is the defense.

documentedHarms.summary: KrebsOnSecurity noted Informed Delivery has been abused by identity thieves who enroll using stolen info; USPS has since added verification.

harmProfile.consumerBenefit: Pre-claiming your address blocks thieves from enrolling and intercepting mail previews.
